item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I don't know why they have condemned all these guys to a very uncomfortable prison. They could keep a few for study and incinerate additional instances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When they say they remanded the interviewee to standard containment, doors that mean the containment described here, or did they out that one in a standard containment cell or something more mundane like that to further interview him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They provide protection from chemical irritants, as well as treatment of some acne, scabbing, and scarring that might otherwise afflict their hosts. Because of this, hosts may become sympathetic to the desires of SCP-1368 instances and agree to assist them.

This is unbelievably ridiculous; I'm not going to let someone/something assume direct control of my body for an indeterminate amount of time in exchange for clearing my acne.

SCP-1368 are vulnerable to high temperatures and are particularly repulsed by fire.

As opposed to us normal people, for whom fire is just a mild irritant?

Aside from that, while I loved the idea and the imagery, the interview was utterly indecipherable and ultimately nothing really happens and there seems to be no reason to be incinerating these things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't seem to be malicious or actively dangerous as well, unless it has the capability to control world leaders and turn them into incomprehensible bloodbags, causing a collapse in society.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: doesnt seem all that keter to me, they have security that can sense it, its objective doesnt seem to be killing people, and its pretty easily stopped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I downvoted as soon as I saw the MTF name, but something kept me reading, and I'm glad I did, and I changed to an upvote by the end. I really like the core concept, and the execution is generally excellent (I like my scips to sound truly scientific, which this does), but I would suggest a few alterations.

First, really do change that MTF name. It's so jarring and cringe-worthy, especially as I imagine the majority of this site's userbase is of a similar demographic to me and therefore used to like Linkin Park, much to my regret.

Secondly, cca. is an extremely uncommon abbreviation for circa; the common abbreviation is c. While cca. is acceptable, it was annoying for me (and may be for others).

Thirdly, the exhalation of air to create wind seems completely unnecessary and tacked-on. When I read it, I thought 'That's interesting, what will he do with that?', but sadly the answer was 'nothing'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has quite a bit of what makes a good article. Strong, logical, containment procedures, a clear description that still allows room for individual imagination, and a insinuated further story ark. The interview really sells this for me, the alien, yet still understandable, syntax and the revelation that they were human. This has a huge amount of potential for development, and is a strong stand alone in upon itself. Upvoted, and one of my personal top five, thank you for putting this up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, my apologies lol… I was unclear. I don't have issues with funny nicknames for the MTFs, but this wasn't "ha ha" funny, but more "Lolwut? no." Funny. Yknow what I mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get kinda disappointed when MTF names turn out to be not very humorous in articles of any kind, but that's probably just personal taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't. I upvoted, but stated my one issue, because having one bit of tacked-on humor in an otherwise straight article seemed out of place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which is making it humorous in this context. Like… so what? Why bother to change it? Who actually cares enough that the MTF name would be a deciding factor on whether or not they find the article as a whole good enough to remain on the site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mind that it's an LP song, cuz I love some of their work, but that song is practically the posterboy for ridiculous, whiny, emotrash music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a fucking Linkin Park reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the problem with this MTF name…? Like, almost all of them are humorous. I'm seriously not understanding the "you need to change this" suggestions going on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Crawling in my Skin

Yes, but do their wounds heal?

Honestly, I liked this one, but that MTF name needs to go. There have to be a good dozen Skinwalker-related names that could work better, a list of suggestions I'd be glad to provide if the author wishes to contact me.

+1 cuz that's the only issue that I have with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF-Phi-10 (AKA "Crawling In My Skin")

Downvoted.

In all seriousness, this just wasn't that interesting to me. Reminds me of that other parasite that takes over your body and uses it as a host.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't like this as much as your others (I loved 1728) but I still like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "hippiespeak"? I don't get it, can you please explain what comes off as "hippiespeak"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've heard of skinwalkers, but this is ridiculous! Just kidding-good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I HAVE BEEN APPEASED!

Actually, good article now. I didn't read the whole thing last time, but the ending presents some great horror, with the "Are they among us?" issue. Also I like the interview, especially the line "friends have a groovy idea".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The benefits aren't a great thing to have in what regard? With regards to the article, or in-universe?

e: You were right. Fixed, hopefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it's like a morph suit putting itself on you without removing your clothes. Thank you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty cool, with implications of even further weirdness underneath being a flying sheet of skin that possesses you. I think one aspect: creating "wind" feels a bit wonky. Is there a precedent for that? If not it feels out of place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't a huge fan of the MTF name, either. After reading it, I braced myself for a lower-quality article. Delightfully surprised, though, and upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm seeing tonal wibbling, or maybe just nonspecific wording (for instance, what sort of skin are they made from?) Also, I kind of feel like the benefits are not a great thing to have, like… fightgood not great. Definitely potential for parasite/body horror here, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Granted it is anomolous but trying to work out how it can wriggle under your cothes and then encapsulate you through a single seam in its chest is making my head hurt. This is like pulling off your underpants without removing your trousers. That said I quite like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF-Phi-10 (AKA "Crawling In My Skin")

They're letting emo teens name the MTF groups now? Tentative initial upvote though, I'm pretty tired so I'll read it again but I didn't really garner much explanation from the interview. Would be nice to know if they have any higher motives than just eating dust.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, but the hippiespeak variant is a little jarring to me. (shrug) Silly, I know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was removed citing confusion about what the line was implying re: other methods. I'll re-add it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There used to be a line about how it would go under people's clothes; I dunno what happened to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An instance of SCP-1368 parasitizes a human host by spreading open its seam, enveloping the host completely and laying over them like a second skin.

…so do they prey exclusively on nudists, or is there some other reason why their manner of predation wouldn't be completely eliminated by wearing clothes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for everyone's help in the chat and fora.
